name: S3 Destination Connector
description: Connection to Amazon S3 or S3-compatible storage for outbound writes (mesh / batch data).
use_cases: Write mesh data to Amazon S3.
S3-compatible object storage (MinIO, etc.) via endpoint_url.

entity.name: Amazon S3 Destination
entity.description: Write data to Amazon S3 or S3-compatible storage in multiple formats with partitioning support.

bucket.help: Required. S3 bucket name (3–63 chars); mirrors S3DestinationConfigSchema.

prefix.help: Object key prefix inside the bucket; default "".

compression.help: Compression; snappy applies to parquet path.

partition_columns.help: For partitioning=custom: array of column names in JSON (see runtime schema).

access_key_id.help: Optional if using IAM role on the worker; else often supplied via connector api_key.

secret_access_key.help: Optional; often supplied via connector api_secret.

force_path_style.help: Boolean in JSON for path-style addressing.

csv_options.help: Object: delimiter, quote, header, escape, null_value (JSON).

connection_config_spec.help: Credentials via api_key/api_secret or IAM-capable deployment; bucket/region/prefix and optional endpoint_url in custom_settings. Runtime uses AWS SDK in platform-backend.

connector_package.description: Connector = connection to object storage; outbound write behavior is implemented in the platform native provider (AWS SDK). Entity, auth, and custom_settings_schema are the declarative contract from this repo.
follow_up_notes: Runtime: S3DestinationConnector — not CatalogRuntimeConnector; write/streaming logic stays in platform-backend until a native recipe runner exists.
custom_settings_schema mirrors S3DestinationConfigSchema for UX/docs; zod in platform-backend remains the runtime validator.
actions/*.json document platform_native operations (not catalog_http_*); dispatcher wiring is future work.
